title: Post-Processing Pipeline
description: Developer plugs in post-processors to enrich or filter records after capture, before they are placed on the change event queue.

actors.name: Developer
actors.description: Configures and implements custom PostProcessors via connector config or SPI

actors.name: Connector (BaseSourceTask)
actors.description: Registers service providers and bootstraps the service registry on startup

actors.name: EventDispatcher
actors.description: Invokes doPostProcessing(key, value) on every captured change record

actors.name: PostProcessorFactory (SPI)
actors.description: Third-party extension point loaded via java.util.ServiceLoader to supply additional processors

use_cases.name: Configure Post-Processors
use_cases.description: Developer sets 'custom.post.processors' in connector config listing processor names. Each name requires a '<name>.type' class and optional '<name>.<option>' properties (new convention: 'custom.post.processors.<name>.*').

use_cases.name: Register PostProcessorRegistryServiceProvider
use_cases.description: BaseSourceTask.registerServiceProviders() calls serviceRegistry.registerServiceProvider(new PostProcessorRegistryServiceProvider()). Registers the provider lazily — the service is only created on first getService() call.

use_cases.name: Load and Instantiate PostProcessors
use_cases.description: DefaultServiceRegistry.getService(PostProcessorRegistry.class) triggers PostProcessorRegistryServiceProvider.createService(). It reads config, instantiates each PostProcessor via reflection (configuration.getInstance(type, PostProcessor.class)), calls postProcessor.configure(properties), then merges any processors from ServiceLoader<PostProcessorFactory>. Returns a PostProcessorRegistry holding the immutable list.

use_cases.name: Inject Dependencies into PostProcessors
use_cases.description: DefaultServiceRegistry.doMultiPhaseInitialization() runs: injectDependencies (scans @InjectService methods + ServiceRegistryAware.injectServiceRegistry), configureService (Configurable.configure), startService (Startable.start). PostProcessorRegistry.start() injects BeanRegistry and ServiceRegistry into each processor that implements BeanRegistryAware / ServiceRegistryAware.

use_cases.name: Apply Post-Processors to Change Record
use_cases.description: EventDispatcher.doPostProcessing(Object key, Struct value) iterates postProcessorRegistry.getProcessors() and calls processor.apply(key, value) on each. The Struct value is still mutable at this stage, enabling enrichment or filtering before the record is enqueued.
use_cases.trigger: Every snapshot or streaming change event dispatched

use_cases.name: Re-select Columns (Built-in PostProcessor)
use_cases.description: Developer enables ReselectColumnsPostProcessor (implements PostProcessor, BeanRegistryAware, ServiceRegistryAware). On apply(), it re-queries the DB via JdbcConnection for columns that contain the unavailable-value placeholder or are null, patching the Struct in-place.

use_cases.name: Supply External PostProcessors via SPI
use_cases.description: Third party implements PostProcessorFactory and registers it in META-INF/services. PostProcessorRegistryServiceProvider uses ServiceLoader<PostProcessorFactory>.findFirst().map(PostProcessorFactory::get) and appends the returned list to the registry.

use_cases.name: Shut Down and Close PostProcessors
use_cases.description: DefaultServiceRegistry.close() iterates registrations in reverse order, calling stopService() which calls Closeable.close() on each. PostProcessorRegistry.close() calls postProcessor.close() for every processor, releasing resources.

key_relationships: Developer configures UC1, enables UC6, implements UC7
BaseSourceTask (Connector) triggers UC2, UC3, UC4, UC8
EventDispatcher triggers UC5 for every change record
UC5 includes UC6 when ReselectColumnsPostProcessor is registered
PostProcessorRegistry implements Service, Startable, Closeable, ServiceRegistryAware
DefaultServiceRegistry implements ServiceRegistry; manages lifecycle of all services
key_classes: PostProcessorRegistry — Service, Startable, Closeable, ServiceRegistryAware; holds List<PostProcessor>; start() injects deps into processors
PostProcessor (interface) — configure(Map), apply(Object key, Struct value), close()
PostProcessorFactory (interface) — get(): List<PostProcessor>; loaded via ServiceLoader
DefaultServiceRegistry — implements ServiceRegistry; ConcurrentMap registrations; getService(), registerServiceProvider(), doMultiPhaseInitialization()
EventDispatcher — holds postProcessorRegistry; doPostProcessing(key, value) iterates processors
ReselectColumnsPostProcessor — concrete PostProcessor; BeanRegistryAware, ServiceRegistryAware; re-selects DB columns on apply()
important_notes: PostProcessors operate on raw mutable Structs before change event queue insertion — unlike Kafka SMTs which operate on serialized records. Dependency injection uses @InjectService annotation scanned by DefaultServiceRegistry. Old config convention is deprecated. ServiceLoader<PostProcessorFactory> SPI allows external processor bundles.
